TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Dead Nations Faction.

Translations.Comment: Direct translation capturing the essence of risen undead kingdoms. Maintains the dark, regal tone.
Direct translation. Basic undead infantry with martial discipline.
Cognate that works in Spanish. Shambling undead hordes with no tactics or discipline.
'Espectral' evokes the spectral wight. Elite warriors bound to ancient tombs with supernatural strength.
Direct translation. Disciplined spear-wielding formations forming the backbone of undead armies.
Captures the gothic imagery of ancient siege weapons exhumed from forgotten tombs.
Composite noun in Spanish. Silent, precise archers of the undead legions.
'Sombríos' conveys supernatural darkness and dread. Cursed beasts bound to the Dead Nations.
Undead cavalry from fallen noble houses. Maintains tactical discipline and knightly bearing in death.
Elite undead cavalry. Their presence inspires supernatural dread on the battlefield.
Dark fantasy flying unit. 'Horribles' emphasizes their monstrous, dreadful nature.
Massive reanimated corpse. Word order follows Spanish adjective placement for impact.
Undead wyrm of terrible power. Evokes the primal dread of skeletal draconic horror.
Ancient undead noble and supreme commander. 'Espectral' conveys otherworldly power and authority.
Undead knight with blood-drinking powers. Combines martial prowess with vampiric sorcery.
Master of death magic and dark sorcery. Standard fantasy term in Spanish, commanding undead legions.
